tasks.title: Sentry error tracking integration
tasks.description:
Install `@sentry/node` and wire Sentry error tracking throughout the API. This is the last P1 production blocker — currently errors are only visible in pino logs, with no external aggregation or alerting.

**Implementation:**

1. `pnpm --filter api add @sentry/node` — install Sentry SDK.

2. **Initialize Sentry in `apps/api/src/index.ts`** — call `Sentry.init()` at the very top of `main()` (before any other code), reading `SENTRY_DSN` from env. If `SENTRY_DSN` is not set, skip initialization gracefully (no crash). Set `environment` to `NODE_ENV`, `tracesSampleRate: 0.1` for production. The `.env.example` already has a commented-out `SENTRY_DSN=` line — uncomment and document it.

3. **Express error handler in `apps/api/src/app.ts`** — the global error handler is at line ~283. Add `Sentry.captureException(err)` before the existing pino log + JSON response. Also add Sentry's `requestHandler()` middleware early in the middleware chain (before routes) and `errorHandler()` after all routes but before the custom error handler.

4. **GraphQL error capture** — in `apps/api/src/app.ts`, the yoga instance has a `plugins` array. Add a custom yoga plugin that captures GraphQL errors via `onExecute` or `onResultProcess`. When `result.errors` exists, call `Sentry.captureException()` for each error, attaching the operation name and variables as context. Skip expected user errors (ERR_NOT_FOUND, ERR_VALIDATION, ERR_AUTH) — only capture unexpected server errors.

5. **AI operation tracking** — in `apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`, wrap the main AI call functions. On failure, call `Sentry.captureException()` with extra context: prompt type, token estimate, model name. This captures AI provider failures (rate limits, timeouts, bad responses).

6. **User context** — in `apps/api/src/graphql/context.ts`, after the user is loaded from JWT, call `Sentry.setUser({ id: user.id, email: user.email })` so errors are associated with specific users.

7. **Flush on shutdown** — in `apps/api/src/index.ts`, in the existing graceful shutdown handler, add `await Sentry.close(2000)` before `process.exit()` to flush pending events.

8. **Source maps** — add `Sentry.init({ integrations: [Sentry.nodeProfilingIntegration()] })` is optional for MVP. At minimum, ensure `dsn`, `environment`, and `release` (from package.json version) are set.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes with @sentry/node installed
pnpm lint passes
When SENTRY_DSN is unset, API starts without errors (graceful skip)
When SENTRY_DSN is set, Sentry.init() is called and errors are captured
GraphQL errors (non-user errors) are sent to Sentry with operation context
AI failures are captured with prompt type and token context
Sentry flushes on graceful shutdown before process.exit()
User context (id, email) is set on each authenticated request

tasks.title: GraphQL query complexity/cost limits
tasks.description:
Add cost-based query complexity limiting to the GraphQL API. Currently only depth limiting (max 10) exists via `depthLimitRule()` in `apps/api/src/graphql/schema.ts`. Depth alone doesn't prevent wide queries that fan out exponentially (e.g., `projects { tasks { comments { author } } }` = 100×100×50 = 500K rows).

**Implementation:**

1. Write a custom complexity analysis validation rule in `apps/api/src/graphql/schema.ts` (alongside the existing `depthLimitRule`). Do NOT install an external package — write a custom AST visitor similar to `depthLimitRule`. The visitor walks the query AST, sums up cost per field:
   - Default field cost: 1
   - List fields (returning arrays): multiply by estimated item count. Use a `costMap` object that assigns multipliers to known list fields:
     - `projects`: 20
     - `tasks`: 50
     - `comments`: 30
     - `activities`: 50
     - `notifications`: 30
     - `sprints`: 10
     - `labels`: 20
     - `epics`: 20
     - `reports`: 10
     - `savedFilters`: 10
     - `webhookEndpoints`: 10
     - `webhookDeliveries`: 50
     - All other list fields: 10 (default multiplier)
   - Nested list costs multiply: `projects(20) > tasks(50)` = 1000
   - Max cost threshold: **10,000** (configurable via `MAX_QUERY_COST` env var, default 10000)

2. **Register the rule** in the `validationRules` array of the yoga config in `apps/api/src/app.ts` (right after `depthLimitRule`).

3. **Error message** — when cost exceeds the limit, return a GraphQL validation error: `"Query complexity cost (${cost}) exceeds maximum allowed (${maxCost}). Simplify your query by requesting fewer nested collections."`

4. **Logging** — log queries that exceed 50% of the cost limit at `warn` level (via the existing pino logger) for monitoring before they hit the hard limit.

5. **Exempt introspection** — `__schema` and `__type` queries should be exempt from cost analysis (cost = 0).

6. **Test manually** — verify that the GraphiQL UI still works (introspection queries pass), normal app queries pass, and a deeply nested fan-out query is rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes
pnpm lint passes
Custom complexity rule rejects queries exceeding 10,000 cost with a clear error message
Normal app queries (single project with tasks, comments) pass under the limit
Introspection queries (__schema, __type) are exempt from cost analysis
Queries exceeding 50% cost limit are logged at warn level
MAX_QUERY_COST is configurable via env var with 10000 default

tasks.title: KanbanBoard task reorder persistence
tasks.description:
The KanbanBoard supports Up/Down arrow keyboard reordering of tasks within a column, but it's local state only — lost on page reload. The Task model already has a `position: Float?` field with an index on `[projectId, sprintId, position]` in `apps/api/prisma/schema/task.prisma`. Wire it up end-to-end.

**Implementation:**

1. **GraphQL mutation** — add `reorderTask(taskId: ID!, position: Float!): Task!` to `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/task.ts`. In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts`, implement the resolver: verify user has access to the task's project, then `prisma.task.update({ where: { id: taskId }, data: { position } })`. Return the updated task.

2. **Position calculation** — use fractional positioning (like Figma/Linear). When moving task B between tasks A and C:
   - `newPosition = (A.position + C.position) / 2`
   - If moving to top: `newPosition = firstTask.position - 1000`
   - If moving to bottom: `newPosition = lastTask.position + 1000`
   - Initial positions for tasks without a position: assign `index * 1000` on first load

3. **Frontend — KanbanBoard.tsx** (`apps/web/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx`):
   - The `handleKeyboardReorder` function (around line 60-80) currently swaps items in local `columnOrder` state. After the swap, compute the new fractional position and call the `reorderTask` mutation.
   - Add `onReorderTask` prop: `(taskId: string, position: number) => Promise<void>`
   - On mount, sort tasks within each column by `position` (nulls last, then by createdAt fallback)

4. **Hook wiring** — in `apps/web/src/hooks/useProjectData.ts` (or the appropriate task CRUD hook), add `handleReorderTask` that calls the GraphQL mutation. Pass it as the `onReorderTask` prop to KanbanBoard.

5. **Drag-and-drop reorder** — the existing drag handlers (`handleDragStart`, `handleDragEnd`) also need to call `onReorderTask` with the computed position after a drop. Currently they only update `sprintColumn` via `onColumnChange`.

6. **Query update** — ensure the `tasks` query in `apps/web/src/utils/taskHelpers.ts` (TASK_FIELDS) includes the `position` field so it's fetched.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes
pnpm lint passes
reorderTask mutation persists position to DB
Up/Down arrow reorder in KanbanBoard calls the mutation and survives page reload
Drag-and-drop reorder also persists position
Tasks within a column are sorted by position (nulls last, then createdAt)
TASK_FIELDS includes position field

tasks.title: BacklogView sprint picker keyboard UX
tasks.description:
The sprint picker dropdown in BacklogView (rendered in `apps/web/src/components/SprintSection.tsx` around lines 140-175) opens via M key or click but lacks standard keyboard dismissal — no Escape-to-close and no click-outside-to-close. This is a WCAG accessibility gap.

**Implementation:**

1. **Escape key handler** — in `SprintSection.tsx`, add an `onKeyDown` handler to the sprint picker dropdown container. When `Escape` is pressed:
   - Close the dropdown (`setShowSprintPicker(false)` or equivalent state setter)
   - Return focus to the trigger element (the M-key button or the task row)
   - Announce via aria-live: "Sprint picker closed"

2. **Click-outside handler** — add a `useEffect` that listens for `mousedown` events on `document`. If the click target is outside the dropdown ref, close it. Use a `ref` on the dropdown container and check `!ref.current?.contains(e.target)`. Clean up the listener on unmount.

3. **Focus management** — when the dropdown opens, focus the first sprint option. When it closes (via Escape, click-outside, or selection), restore focus to the trigger element. Use `useRef` to store the trigger element before opening.

4. **ARIA attributes** — ensure the dropdown has:
   - `role="listbox"` on the sprint list
   - `role="option"` on each sprint item
   - `aria-expanded` on the trigger button
   - `aria-activedescendant` tracking the focused option

5. **Arrow key navigation** — within the open dropdown, Up/Down arrows should move between sprint options (if not already implemented). Enter/Space selects the focused sprint.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes
pnpm lint passes
Escape key closes the sprint picker dropdown and restores focus to trigger
Clicking outside the dropdown closes it
Opening the dropdown focuses the first sprint option
Dropdown has proper ARIA: role=listbox, role=option, aria-expanded, aria-activedescendant
Up/Down arrows navigate between sprint options in the open dropdown

tasks.title: Lazy-load heavy components + route error boundaries
tasks.description:
Several large components are eagerly imported in `apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx` and loaded even when not rendered. Additionally, `apps/web/src/App.tsx` has a single global `ErrorBoundary` around all routes — one failing lazy chunk crashes the entire app with no per-route recovery. **Implementation:** 1. **Lazy-load heavy modals in ProjectDetail.tsx** — convert these eager imports to `React.lazy()`: - `GanttChart` (~432 lines) — only rendered when timeline view is active - `ProjectSettingsModal` (~794 lines) — only rendered when settings modal is open - `TaskPlanApprovalDialog` (~388 lines) — only rendered during AI plan approval - `CloseSprintModal` (~320 lines) — only rendered when closing a sprint - `BatchCodeGenModal` — only rendered during batch code generation Wrap each lazy component's render site with `
}>`. Since these are modals/panels that appear on user action, the spinner fallback is brief and acceptable. 2. **Route-level error boundaries in App.tsx** — currently all routes share one `` wrapper. Create a `RouteErrorBoundary` component (can be inline in App.tsx or in `components/shared/`) that: - Catches errors from a single route's lazy chunk - Shows a friendly error message: "Something went wrong loading this page." - Includes a "Reload" button that calls `window.location.reload()` - Includes a "Go Home" link back to `/` - Wrap each `` element's component: `` 3. **Chunk load retry** — add a `lazyWithRetry` helper that wraps `React.lazy()` to retry failed chunk loads (network blips). On import failure, wait 1 second, then retry up to 2 times before giving up. Pattern: ```ts function lazyWithRetry(importFn, retries = 2) { return React.lazy(() => importFn().catch(err => { if (retries > 0) return new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1000)).then(() => importFn()); throw err; })); } ```
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes
pnpm lint passes
pnpm build succeeds (lazy chunks are split correctly by Vite)
GanttChart, ProjectSettingsModal, TaskPlanApprovalDialog, CloseSprintModal are lazy-loaded
Each route has its own ErrorBoundary — one failing route doesn't crash others
RouteErrorBoundary shows reload + go home options
lazyWithRetry retries failed chunk imports up to 2 times

tasks.title: Template UX improvements — dropdown close + form fields
tasks.description:
Two template UX gaps: (1) the template dropdown menu in ProjectDetail doesn't close on Escape or click-outside, and (2) the create/edit template forms in ProjectSettingsModal are missing fields that the model supports.

**Part 1: Template dropdown close behavior** (`apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx`)

The template dropdown is around lines 481-520, toggled by `showTemplateMenu` state. Currently it only closes via an explicit "Close" button (line ~520) or when a template is selected.

- Add `onKeyDown` handler: close on Escape key, restore focus to the template button
- Add click-outside handler: `useEffect` with `mousedown` listener on document, check if click target is outside the dropdown ref
- Add `ref` to the dropdown container div

**Part 2: Template form fields** (`apps/web/src/components/ProjectSettingsModal.tsx`)

The create template form (around lines 499-535) currently only has: name, description, priority, taskType. The `TaskTemplate` model also supports: `instructions`, `acceptanceCriteria`, `estimatedHours`, `storyPoints`. The edit form (around lines 458-489) has the same gap.

- Add `tplInstructions` textarea field — label: "Instructions", placeholder: "Implementation details...", 4 rows
- Add `tplAcceptanceCriteria` textarea field — label: "Acceptance Criteria", placeholder: "How to verify this task is done...", 3 rows
- Add `tplEstimatedHours` number input — label: "Estimated Hours", min 0, step 0.5
- Add `tplStoryPoints` number input — label: "Story Points", min 0, step 1
- Wire these fields into both the create and edit mutations (the GraphQL mutations already accept these fields)
- Wire into the "Save as Template" flow too — when saving an existing task as a template, pre-fill instructions and acceptanceCriteria from the task

Both parts should be in one commit since they're both template UX fixes.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes
pnpm lint passes
Template dropdown closes on Escape key press
Template dropdown closes on click outside
Create template form includes instructions, acceptanceCriteria, estimatedHours, storyPoints fields
Edit template form includes the same additional fields
Save as Template pre-fills instructions and acceptanceCriteria from the source task
All new fields are wired into the GraphQL mutations
